mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >70% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in >60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses predictive power when controlled for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, which reduces input cost pressures and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive returns during tightening periods, with a negative Rank IC over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 285 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >65% of periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or the signal decays to zero predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, boosting consumer spending expectations and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal generates negative 20-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly gross domestic product growth reflects underlying economic expansion that supports corporate earnings trajectories, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows a negative or zero correlation with 60-day forward returns across all tested regimes, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in industrial production signals immediate supply chain bottlenecks and manufacturing contraction, triggering risk aversion and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation expectations are controlled.
